text: Setup took forever. Didn't understand what to input first — income before or after tax? No guidance.
The onboarding wizard is confusing. I had to restart twice because I entered the wrong expense category.
First launch: no explanation of what the app does. Threw me straight into a form. Uninstalled and reinstalled.
I'm a freelancer. The income field only had monthly salary. Doesn't fit my situation at all.
Took me 20 minutes to get through setup. That's too long. After that it's fine.
No progress indicator during onboarding. I had no idea how many more steps were left.
Onboarding flow is okay but there are too many fields upfront. Would be better to start simple.
The expense categories don't match how I think about money. Discretionary vs Fixed isn't intuitive.
Crashed during onboarding. Lost all my data. Had to start over.
The initial setup needs a short video or tooltip tour. Just text labels isn't enough.
Entering expenses manually every time is exhausting. I stopped using it after a week.
The manual entry is the main bottleneck. If it connected to my bank I'd use it daily.
I have to re-enter my numbers every session. There's no persistence. Frustrating.
Can't copy-paste from my bank statement. Have to type each number manually. This is 2024.
Manual entry is tedious but I get it — no bank sync. Would love a CSV import at least.
I entered wrong numbers once and couldn't find where to edit them later. Had to redo everything.
No recurring expense memory. I enter rent every single time. Please remember my fixed costs.
Data doesn't save between sessions. I close the app and everything is gone. What's the point?
Would be 5 stars if it connected to Plaid. Manual entry kills the habit loop.
The projections look too optimistic. Assumes I'll hit my savings goal every month. I won't.
I don't trust the AI recommendations. No explanation of how the numbers are calculated.
The scenario engine is interesting but I wish I knew what assumptions it's making.
It told me I'd save $8k by cutting dining by $200/month. That math doesn't add up.
Love the concept but projections feel like a black box. Show your work.
The baseline scenario assumes best case. I need a pessimistic scenario option.
No bank sync is a dealbreaker for me. I use Copilot for this reason.
Plaid integration would be a game changer. Right now it's too much manual work.
Competitor apps connect to my bank. This one doesn't. Hard to recommend.
When is bank sync coming? That's the one thing stopping me from going premium.
Great logic but needs bank connection. I'll wait for that feature before recommending to friends.
No weekly check-in reminder. I forget to use it. Needs a nudge system.
I wish it sent me an alert when I'm about to exceed a spending category.
No push notifications. Out of sight out of mind. I used it twice and forgot about it.
Would love a monthly review email. Like a here's how your month went summary.
Good tool but I need reminders to actually use it consistently. Habit loop is missing.
Free tier only gives 2 scenarios. That's not enough to actually test anything.
Paywall hits too early. I didn't see enough value before being asked to upgrade.
$15/month is too expensive for a tool without bank sync. Doesn't justify the price.
There should be a 14-day free trial of premium. I can't tell if it's worth it without trying.
Clean design. The dark mode looks great. Charts are easy to read.
UI is polished but the scenario comparison screen is hard to parse with too many numbers.
Best-looking finance app I've used. Very clean.
Love the interface. The goal progress bar is motivating.
